D1 OpticsL3 ApplyReference

Curved Mirror Foci — Concave vs Convex

For spherical mirror of radius R, focal length f = R/2; concave (R>0) focuses parallel rays to a real focal point; convex (R<0) diverges them from a virtual focal point

Mirror equation: 1/d_o + 1/d_i = 2/R = 1/f. Magnification m = −d_i/d_o. Concave: object beyond f gives inverted real image; inside f gives upright virtual image (shaving mirror). Convex: always upright virtual diminished image (passenger-side car mirror, security mirror).

Slide object distance d_o; mirror is concave R=+150 px by default.
